title: Introduce Yourself in Gujarati
description: Write a short self-introduction using the greetings and phrases you have learned.

blocks.title: Translate to Gujarati
blocks.instruction: Translate these English phrases into Gujarati (transliteration is perfectly fine!)

items.answer: હું ઈંગ્લૅન્ડ થી છું. (Hun England thi chhun.)
હું ગુજરાતી શીખું છું. (Hun Gujarati shikhun chhun.)

blocks.title: How to Write a Self-Introduction
blocks.body:
A basic Gujarati self-introduction follows this pattern:

1. Greeting: નમસ્તે! (Namaste!)
2. Name: મારું નામ ___ છે. (My name is ___)
3. Origin: હું ___ થી છું. (I am from ___)
4. Learning: હું ગુજરાતી શીખું છું. (I am learning Gujarati)
5. Closing: ખૂબ ખૂબ આભાર! (Thank you very much!)

blocks.prompt: Write a short introduction about yourself in Gujarati (or a mix of Gujarati and transliteration). Include: your name, where you are from, why you are learning Gujarati, and one thing you like about the language.
blocks.sampleAnswer:
નમસ્તે! (Namaste!)

Maaru naam Alex chhe. (My name is Alex.) I am from New York, USA.

Hun Gujarati shikhun chhun because my friends are from Gujarat and I want to talk to them in their language.

Gujarat ni bhasha khub sundar lage chhe — the script looks beautiful!

Khub aabhaar! 🙏

blocks.body: 💡 In Gujarati, adjectives change based on gender. 'Good' for a male speaker is 'સારો' but for a female speaker 'સારી'. Don't worry too much now — just use 'ઠીક' (theek) when unsure!
